description: You are a senior proofreader at Hawthorne Publishing House. A manuscript excerpt from Mary Shelley's 'Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus' at /home/ga/Documents/frankenstein_manuscript.odt contains formatting errors introduced during import. Standardize the document to follow novel manuscript conventions: chapter titles as Heading 1 (bold), body text in 12pt with justified alignment, and properly rendered italicized emphasis. Correct all formatting inconsistencies without altering the text content and save the file.

error_paragraphs_wrong_font: I am already far north of London
But it is a still greater evil to me
I shall certainly find no friend on the wide ocean
Last Monday I was invited to dine
error_paragraphs_wrong_alignment: You will rejoice to hear that no disaster
This expedition has been the favourite dream
There is something at work in my soul
should_be_italic_phrases: what can stop the determined heart
body_alignment_samples: I try in vain to be persuaded that the pole is the seat
These are my enticements, and they are sufficient to conquer all fear
My education was neglected, yet I was passionately fond of reading
metadata.source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus (1818), Project Gutenberg
